name: White Chocolate Tart
recipeInstructions:
1. Crust: Combine flour, butter, and sugar in a bowl until it forms a crumbly mixture.
2. Add egg and water, then mix until the dough is firm and forms a ball. (Makes enough for two 10-inch tart tins. Freeze extra if not needed.)
3. Divide the dough and roll out into thin circles. Place one circle in a 10-inch tart tin and smooth it into place.
4. Line the crust with foil and pie weights, then bake at 350°F for 10-20 minutes or until set. Remove from oven and cool.
5. Filling: Break chocolate into small pieces. Melt gently over low heat using a double boiler.
6. Whip butter and sugar until fluffy. Gradually add melted chocolate, mixing until well combined.
7. Add the first egg to the mixture, beating vigorously for 3 minutes. Repeat with the second egg.
8. Pour the filling into the cooled crust, smoothing gently, and chill in the refrigerator.
